description.value:
Specially treated wrappings made from stone-wool — mineral fibers heated and pressed into dense, insulating mats that can be worn over armor or under cloaks. Grey-white woven fiber, warm to the touch, surprisingly light and flexible. Each set is sized for one wearer. Crafted by Heitfolk artisans for deep-route expeditions through the extremes of the White Waste.

While worn, these wrappings reduce all cold damage by 3 and preserve body heat with remarkable efficiency. Any character wearing them is visibly identified as allied with the Heitfolk.

DM Notes

ACQUISITION: Automatic from Hildvarð Bruneson at the Steam-Oasis (Beat 20). Four sets — one per hero. Hildvarð says: "You'll need them for what's ahead. The cold is getting worse, and you're carrying warmth the world is hungry for."

MECHANICS (while worn):

  • Cold Resistance 3 — reduces all cold damage by 3 (environmental cold, frost attacks, and cold-based abilities).
  • Heat Preservation: Cold-based Heat consumption is reduced by 1 for the wearer.
  • Heitfolk-made: Crafted specifically for the extremes of the White Waste. Any character wearing these wrappings is identified as allied with the Heitfolk.

DURATION: Lasts for the remainder of Act 3. Heitfolk craftsmanship is built for deep-route expeditions. The mineral oil slowly depletes, but at this pace the wraps remain effective through the campaign's conclusion.

NARRATIVE: The Cold-Wraps have no further relevance after Grafvitnir is sealed. The cold retreats. The wraps are warm relics of a colder time.
